content: The CRA Scientific Research and Experimental Development (SR&ED) program provides refundable investment tax credits for qualifying research and development activities. Eligible work includes: development of clean technology processes, systematic investigation for emissions reduction, process improvement experiments for sustainability, and development of environmental monitoring systems. Canadian-controlled private corporations (CCPCs) can claim an enhanced 35% refundable tax credit on the first $3 million of qualifying expenditures. For food and beverage companies, qualifying activities may include: development of low-carbon cooking processes, waste reduction research, sustainable packaging development, and energy efficiency optimization. Estimated eligibility for a mid-sized food service company with active R&D: up to $64,000 in refundable credits.
